binary-tree-max-path-sum.statement: Given a binary tree with possibly negative values, print the **maximum path sum** of any non-empty path (a path may start and end at any node, need not pass through the root).

binary-tree-zigzag-level-order.statement: Given a binary tree, print its **zigzag level-order traversal**: one line per depth level, values space-separated; level 0 reads left-to-right, level 1 right-to-left, level 2 left-to-right, and so on, alternating every level.

bitonic-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Bitonic Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

bucket-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Bucket Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

catalan-number.statement: Given `n`, print the **n-th Catalan number** C(n).

cocktail-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Cocktail Shaker Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

comb-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Comb Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

combination-sum-iv-count.statement: Given a set of distinct positive integers `nums` and a `target`, print the number of **ordered sequences** (elements may repeat, order matters) drawn from `nums` that sum to `target`.

count-tree-nodes.statement: Given a binary tree, print the **total number of nodes**.

counting-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Counting Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

course-schedule-feasible.statement: There are `n` courses labeled `0..n-1`. You are given `m` prerequisite pairs `(a, b)` meaning course `b` must be completed before course `a`. Print `true` if it is possible to finish **all** courses, or `false` if the prerequisites form a cycle that makes this impossible.

cycle-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Cycle Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

decode-ways.statement: A digit string can be decoded into letters via `'1'->'A', ..., '26'->'Z'`. Given a digit string `s`, print the **number of ways** to decode it (0 if none — e.g. a stray leading zero in a group).

diameter-of-binary-tree.statement: Given a binary tree, print the **diameter** — the length (in edges) of the longest path between any two nodes.

distinct-subsequences.statement: Given strings `s` and `t`, print the number of **distinct subsequences** of `s` that equal `t`.

distinct-subsets-count.statement: Given a multiset of `n` integers (it may contain duplicates), print the number of **distinct subsets** of it, counting the empty subset. Two subsets are the same if they contain the same multiset of values regardless of which original duplicate index they came from.

evaluate-reverse-polish-notation.statement: Given a Reverse Polish Notation expression as `n` space-separated tokens, print its integer result. Division truncates toward zero.

fast-power.statement: Given `base` and a non-negative integer `exp`, print `base^exp` **exactly** (arbitrary precision), computed via fast exponentiation by squaring in O(log exp) multiplications.

fibonacci-dp.statement:
Given `n`, return the n-th Fibonacci number where F(0)=0, F(1)=1, F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).

Solve it in **O(n)** time and **O(1)** space.

find-all-anagrams-in-string.statement: Given strings `s` and `p`, print all **starting indices** (ascending, space-separated) in `s` where a permutation (anagram) of `p` occurs. Print an empty line if there are none.

fractional-knapsack.statement: Given `n` items with `weights` and `values`, and a knapsack `capacity`, print the **maximum total value** achievable, where fractional amounts of an item may be taken. Print the answer rounded to **2 decimal places**.

gnome-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Gnome Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

heap-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Heap Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

huffman-coding.statement: Given the frequencies of `n` symbols, print the **total weighted path length** (sum of frequency × code length) of an optimal Huffman encoding — the total number of bits used to encode the whole message.

insertion-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Insertion Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

interleaving-strings.statement: Given strings `s1`, `s2`, `s3`, print `true` if `s3` is formed by an **interleaving** of `s1` and `s2` (preserving each string's relative character order), else `false`.

invert-tree-preorder.statement: Given a binary tree, invert it (swap every left/right child) and print the **preorder traversal** of the result, space-separated.

is-balanced-binary-tree.statement: Given a binary tree, print `true` if it is **height-balanced** (every node's two subtree heights differ by at most 1), else `false`.

is-bipartite.statement: Given an undirected graph with `n` nodes (0-indexed) and `m` edges (possibly disconnected), print `true` if the graph is **bipartite** (2-colorable so no edge connects two same-colored nodes), else `false`.

is-symmetric-tree.statement: Given a binary tree, print `true` if it is a **mirror image** of itself around its center, else `false`.

is-valid-bst.statement: Given a binary tree, print `true` if it satisfies the **binary search tree** property, else `false`.

karatsuba.statement: Given two non-negative integers `a` and `b`, print their **exact product**, computed via Karatsuba's divide-and-conquer multiplication.

knapsack-01.statement: Given `n` items with `weights` and `values`, and a knapsack `capacity`, print the **maximum total value** achievable picking each item at most once.

kth-smallest-in-bst.statement: Given a **binary search tree** and an integer `k`, print the `k`-th smallest value in it (1-indexed).

level-order-traversal.statement: Given a binary tree, print its **level-order traversal**: one line per depth level, values space-separated top to bottom.

linear-search.statement:
Given an array `nums` and a target, return the **index** of the first occurrence of `target`, or **-1** if not found.

No sorting is guaranteed.

lowest-common-ancestor-binary-tree.statement: Given a **general binary tree** (no ordering property assumed) and two distinct values `p` and `q` guaranteed to exist in it, print the value of their **lowest common ancestor** — the deepest node that has both `p` and `q` as descendants (a node is considered a descendant of itself).
lowest-common-ancestor-binary-tree.constraints: ["2 \u2264 number of nodes \u2264 10^4", "all values distinct", "p != q"]

lowest-common-ancestor-bst.statement: Given a **binary search tree** and two values `p`, `q` known to exist in it, print the value of their **lowest common ancestor**.

matrix-exponentiation.statement: Given an `n x n` matrix, an exponent `k`, and a `mod`, print `matrix^k mod mod` (each row on its own line, space-separated), computed via fast matrix exponentiation by squaring in O(n^3 log k).

max-distance-to-zero.statement: Given a grid of 0s and 1s (at least one 0), print the **maximum, over all cells**, of the 4-directional distance from that cell to the nearest 0 cell.
max-distance-to-zero.constraints: ["1 \u2264 rows, cols \u2264 20", "at least one cell is 0"]

maximal-rectangle.statement: Given a `rows x cols` binary matrix (only `0`/`1` entries), print the **area of the largest rectangle** that contains only `1`s. Solve by treating each row as the base of a histogram (accumulating consecutive 1-heights column-by-column from the row above) and applying the largest-rectangle-in-histogram technique to each row's histogram.

maximum-product-subarray.statement: Given an integer array `nums`, print the **largest product** of a contiguous, non-empty subarray.

median-of-medians.statement: Given an array `nums` and an integer `k`, print the **k-th smallest** element (1-indexed), computed via the median-of-medians selection algorithm in guaranteed O(n) worst-case time.

median-of-two-sorted-arrays.statement: Given two sorted integer arrays `nums1` (length `m`) and `nums2` (length `n`), print the **median** of the combined sorted array without fully materializing and sorting the merge (aim for O(log(min(m, n))) time). Print the median as a plain integer if `m + n` is odd, otherwise print it with **exactly one decimal place** (e.g. `3.0`, `2.5`).

merge-k-sorted-lists.statement: Given `k` sorted singly-linked lists (each represented as its length followed by its values, space-separated, one list per line), print the **single fully merged sorted list**, space-separated. Use a min-heap based k-way merge for O(N log k) time, where N is the total number of nodes.

merge-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Merge Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

odd-even-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Odd-Even Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

pancake-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Pancake Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

path-sum-exists.statement: Given a binary tree and an integer `target`, print `true` if some **root-to-leaf path** sums exactly to `target`, else `false`.

patience-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Patience Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

polynomial-multiplication.statement: Given two polynomials as integer coefficient lists `a` and `b` (`a[i]` = coefficient of x^i), print the coefficient list of their **product**, space-separated, computed via divide-and-conquer (Karatsuba-style) polynomial multiplication.

quick-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Quick Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

radix-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Radix Sort (LSD)** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

regular-expression-matching.statement: Given a string `s` of lowercase letters and a pattern `p` of lowercase letters, `.`, and `*`, print `true` if `p` matches the **entire** `s`, else `false`. Matching rules: `.` matches any single character; `*` matches **zero or more occurrences of the element immediately preceding it** (a letter or `.`) — this is regex-style repetition, not shell-glob wildcard matching.

remove-k-digits.statement: Given a non-negative integer `num` as a string and an integer `k`, print the **smallest possible number** (no leading zeros, as a string) after removing exactly `k` digits.

reverse-bits.statement: Given a 32-bit unsigned integer `n`, print the integer obtained by **reversing its bits**.

right-side-view.statement: Given a binary tree, print the value of the **rightmost node** visible at each depth level, from top to bottom, space-separated.

rotate-image-90.statement: Given an `N x N` matrix, print the matrix **rotated 90 degrees clockwise**, one row per line, space-separated values.

rotten-oranges-minutes.statement: A grid has cells: `0` empty, `1` fresh orange, `2` rotten orange. Every minute, a rotten orange rots each 4-directionally adjacent fresh orange. Print the **number of minutes** until no fresh orange remains, or **-1** if some fresh orange can never rot.

run-length-encoding.statement: Given a string `s`, print its **run-length encoding**: each maximal run of a repeated character becomes `<count><char>` (e.g. `aaabbc` → `3a2b1c`). Print an empty line for an empty string.

same-tree.statement: Given two binary trees, print `true` if they are **structurally identical** with the same node values, else `false`.

search-2d-matrix.statement: Given an `m x n` matrix where each row is sorted ascending and each row's first integer is greater than the previous row's last integer, print `true` if `target` exists in the matrix, else `false`. Solve in **O(log(m*n))** time by treating the matrix as one flattened sorted array.

selection-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Selection Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

set-matrix-zeroes.statement: Given an `R x C` matrix, if any cell equals `0`, set its entire row and entire column to `0`. Print the resulting matrix, one row per line, space-separated values.

shell-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Shell Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

shortest-path-binary-matrix.statement: Given an `n x n` grid of 0 (open) and 1 (blocked) cells, print the length (in number of cells) of the shortest **8-directional** path from the top-left to the bottom-right cell, or **-1** if none exists.

sieve-of-eratosthenes.statement: Given `n`, print **all prime numbers ≤ n**, space-separated in ascending order, using the Sieve of Eratosthenes. Print an empty line if there are none.

sliding-window-maximum.statement: Given an integer array `nums` and window size `k`, print the **maximum value** of every contiguous window of size `k` as it slides from left to right, space-separated in order.

spiral-matrix-traversal.statement: Given an `R x C` matrix, print all its elements in **clockwise spiral order** (outermost ring first, spiraling inward), space-separated.

stable-matching.statement: Given `n` men and `n` women, each with a fully-ranked preference list over the other side (`men_prefs[i]`/`women_prefs[i]`, most preferred first), print the **man-optimal stable matching** produced by the Gale-Shapley algorithm (men propose): for each man `0..n-1`, print his assigned woman's index, space-separated.

stooge-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Stooge Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

strand-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Strand Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

strassen.statement: Given matrix `A` (`n x m`) and matrix `B` (`m x p`), print their product `A x B` (each row on its own line, space-separated), computed via Strassen's divide-and-conquer algorithm.

string-hashing.statement: Given a string `s` and an integer `k`, print the number of **distinct substrings of length exactly `k`**. Solve using a rolling hash to compare substrings in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.

suffix-array.statement: Given a string `s`, print its **suffix array**: the starting indices of all suffixes of `s`, sorted lexicographically by suffix, space-separated.

sum-root-to-leaf-numbers.statement: Given a binary tree with single-digit node values, treat each root-to-leaf path as a decimal number; print the **sum over all such numbers**.

tim-sort.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Tim Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

unique-paths.statement: A robot starts at the top-left of an `m × n` grid and wants to reach the bottom-right corner. It can only move right or down. Return the number of unique paths.

wildcard-matching.statement: Given a string `s` and a wildcard pattern `p` (`'?'` matches any single character, `'*'` matches any sequence of characters, including the empty sequence), print `true` if `p` matches the **entire** `s`, else `false`.

word-break-count-ways.statement: Given a string `s` and a dictionary of words `wordDict` (one line, space separated), print the number of **distinct ways** to segment `s` into a space-separated sequence of dictionary words.

word-ladder-length.statement: Given `beginWord`, `endWord`, and a `wordList` (one-line, space separated), print the **number of words** in the shortest transformation sequence from `beginWord` to `endWord` (inclusive of both), changing exactly one letter per step, each intermediate word must be in `wordList`. Print **0** if no such sequence exists.
word-ladder-length.constraints: ["1 \u2264 word length \u2264 10", "all words the same length, lowercase"]

word-search-grid.statement: Given an `rows x cols` grid of lowercase letters and a target `word`, print `true` if `word` can be built by moving between **orthogonally adjacent** cells (up, down, left, right) without reusing the same cell twice, else `false`.

z-algorithm.statement: Given a string `s`, print its **Z-array**: `Z[i]` is the length of the longest substring starting at `i` that is also a prefix of `s` (by convention `Z[0] = 0`), space-separated.
